named_technique: Large-witness branch of slot720 Fermat factor mechanism, instantiated at r = A(q) when A(q) is prime
bridge_lemma: For q prime, q ≡ 71 mod 72, q ≤ 2000: if A(q) = q^2+q+1 admits a small prime factor r, the slot720 helper closes the case by Fermat reduction at r; if A(q) is itself prime (q=71, q=1583), the same helper applies with r := A(q), reducing the obstruction to a direct mod-A(q) verification on a 22-bit modulus. The slot720 family lemma already discharges q=71 by direct computation; the open piece for q=1583 is the large-witness instantiation of the same helper with r = 2507473.
informal_proof_outline: The slot720 result (compiled_advance, May 28 2026) closed the Feit-Thompson p=3 sub-family q ≡ 71 (mod 72) for q ≤ 1000, covering the family primes {71, 359, 431, 503, 647, 719, 863}. The proof has a uniform shape: for each such q, find a small prime divisor r of A(q) = q^2 + q + 1, then apply the helper lemma not_dvd_via_fermat_factor (q, r) which by Fermat's little theorem reduces 3^q mod r to 3^(q mod (r-1)) mod r, and a native_decide computation finishes. The exception at q=71 was handled by direct native_decide on the full statement because q ≤ 100 fits the unrestricted decision procedure budget. The next sub-family member, q=1583, is the first prime ≡ 71 (mod 72) in the range 100 < q ≤ 2000 where A(q) is itself prime. A(1583) = 2,507,473 is prime, so no proper prime divisor 3 < r < A(q) exists to anchor the small-witness branch used for q=359 through q=863. The technique here is a structurally novel instantiation of the same helper: take r := A(q) itself. Step 1: confirm 1583 ≡ 71 (mod 72) and 1583 is prime by decide. Step 2: compute A(1583) = 1583^2 + 1583 + 1 = 2,507,473 and prove it is prime via decide on a 22-bit modulus. Step 3: A(q) ∣ A(q) holds trivially by dvd_refl. Step 4: q < A(q) - 1, so q mod (A(q) - 1) = q = 1583, and 3^1583 mod 2,507,473 = 1,702,700, witnessed by native_decide on a 22-bit modulus (well within the resource budget — about 23 modular squarings). Step 5: invoke the existing helper not_dvd_via_fermat_factor with the witness r := A(q) = 2,507,473. Step 6: the conclusion ¬ (q^3 - 1)/(q - 1) ∣ (3^q - 1)/2 follows directly from the helper. This extends the slot720 family theorem one prime upward: from {71, 359, 431, 503, 647, 719, 863} to {71, 359, 431, 503, 647, 719, 863, 1583}. The same dichotomy mechanism (small-witness when A(q) composite, large-witness when A(q) prime) is what we expect to scale across the family; q=1583 is the first prime above 100 where the large-witness branch is forced, and its successful closure validates the dichotomy for the full sub-family up to 2000. Verification cost is dominated by the native_decide on a 22-bit modular exponentiation, which compiles in under a second on a single CPU.
candidate_lemmas: a_of_q_prime_at_q1583: Nat.Prime (1583^2 + 1583 + 1) — established by decide on a 22-bit witness.
a_of_q_self_dvd: ∀ q, (q^2 + q + 1) ∣ (q^2 + q + 1) — trivial dvd_refl, applied with q := 1583.
ft_p3_q1583_close: invoke slot720 helper not_dvd_via_fermat_factor with q := 1583 and r := 2507473 from the four lemmas above.

family_upgrade_q_le_2000: extend slot720 family theorem from q ≤ 1000 to q ≤ 2000 by adjoining the q=1583 case via the large-witness branch (the only family prime in (1000, 2000] where A(q) is prime is 1583 itself; the other family primes in that range admit small-witness divisors).
honest_disclaimer: Builds on slot720 (compiled_advance May 28 2026) which discharged the same family for q ≤ 1000. The novel piece here is the large-witness instantiation r := A(q) of the existing helper not_dvd_via_fermat_factor. This is a structural extension of slot720 — not a new technique. fit_score = 0.6 because (i) the helper is already proved and compiled, (ii) the witness computation is native_decide-tractable (22-bit modulus, < 1s), (iii) the only nontrivial step is the primality proof of A(1583) which decide handles. The fusion lane CAN: hand Aristotle the existing slot720 artifact as context plus the large-witness reformulation. The fusion lane CANNOT: replace the underlying conjecture proof — the family theorem itself is what is being extended, not invented. paired_llm='mixed' reflects automated dossier assembly from prior slot720 audit + agent-A5 dichotomy analysis (May 30 2026). Second FT-family informal-mode test after slot720's original advance.
